query: extract the internet complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Is Your Internet Connection BROKEN? Fix it Fast! - YouTube
results.content:
It is beyond frustrating when your internet connection works one minute then stops the next. Internet connection sometimes can be intermittent or just completely broken. Sometimes it's because of a malware infection, a virus, or even just a simple Windows configuration problem. If you've already tried disconnecting from your wireless connection and reconnecting, restarting your computer and your internet router, and you continue to have these problems, I got you covered. It's a super easy fix with no skill involved. In today's video, I'm going to show you how to reset the Windows socket. We're going to do this using the NetShell command. Don't worry, it's complicated, but I'm going to make it super easy. You don't have to be connected to the internet to run this utility, and it only takes [...] 💢 Virus/malware cleaning software ---

results.title: 10 Common Reasons Why Internet Fail and What ITs Can Do About It
results.content: Internet fail can be incredibly frustrating, disrupting work, entertainment, and connectivity. When we rely on the internet for so much, experiencing an interruption can feel like a major setback. Common reasons for internet failures include issues with the ISP, router problems, Wi-Fi signal interference, outdated hardware or software, and even environmental factors like weather conditions. To tackle these problems effectively, IT professionals often start by diagnosing the issue and checking for any service outages or disruptions from the ISP. They may then troubleshoot the router and Wi-Fi settings, ensuring they’re configured correctly and updating firmware if necessary. Additionally, they might recommend using Ethernet instead of Wi-Fi for more stable connections or investing in a

results.title: Why Is My Internet Not Working & How Do I Fix It? - Avast
results.content:
### Internet service provider (ISP) outages

Your internet service provider’s (ISP) connection may go down due to maintenance work or unexpected issues. The best way to confirm whether there’s an outage is by checking your ISP’s website or contacting them directly. If you’re unsure who your ISP is, you can check your internet bill or use an online tool like WhatIsMyISP to see which provider is associated with your IP address.

If the problem is indeed an ISP outage, you’ll need to wait for the provider to resolve it. In the meantime, you can use mobile data or connect to another available network.

### Low internet speed

If your internet is working super slowly, it can be really frustrating. It may even be bad enough that it seems like your internet’s not working at all. [...] ## How to fix internet connection issues

There are several proactive steps you can take to fix your internet connection issues at home. A simple first step is restarting your router, but other fixes include changing your network settings, replacing hardware, or disconnecting unused devices.

1. Restart your router or modem
2. Check for internet outages
3. Check hardware
4. Change broken wires and cables
5. Check network settings
6. Reset your browser settings
7. Remove some connected devices
8. Encrypt your internet traffic

### Restart your router or modem

Your router may not be connecting to the internet because of a buildup of temporary files, which can cause network connection issues. Restarting your router or modem usually fixes these problems by clearing temporary files. [...] ## Is the issue really with your internet connection?

There are checks you can do to determine if your internet problems are due to your Wi-Fi connection or another issue, such as a download cap from your ISP, malware, or faults with the service you're trying to access.

### Check your internet service plan

Examine the specifics of your internet plan to help pinpoint any issues.

 Verify your data allowance: If you don’t have an unlimited internet plan, you may have exhausted your data limit, which can cause a slow or cut-off connection.
 Review your internet speed: Use free online tools like Speedtest.net to compare your current speeds with what your plan promises. A significant difference may indicate an issue, such as network congestion or throttling.

#### Internet Issues on the Complaint Form

Availability  
Services are not available in your area, issues with coverage areas or coverage maps.

Billing  
Issues with your provider about advertised rates, service charges, taxes, fees, surcharges, lifeline.

Equipment  
Issues with your modem, router, or other Internet equipment and related equipment.

Interference:  
Issues with interference.

Privacy  
Your personal information has been breached.

Speed

Issues with your Internet speeds, including not receiving advertised speeds or latency, issues.

If our consumer guides do not address your issue, another option is to file a complaint.

results.title: How To Diagnose Internet Connectivity Problems
results.content:
### Contact ISP or Network Administrator

If you are confident that your software configuration is in check and have tried resetting the network equipment, its always possible that the external uplink is having issues. Try contacting the nearest network administrator if this is a business or commercial setting, or the service provider in a residential setting.

## Device Drivers: re-install network device drivers (Windows)

At this point we have ruled out most of the common software issues that you are likely to ever see. Its possible now that either the hardware itself is broken or the driver has been messed up in some manner. The best way to resolve this is to download the latest official driver from the manufacturer and install it. [...] If the internet connection is working fine in safe mode and not in regular mode, there is a decent chance that certain software is blocking the connection, because said software will not be running in safe mode. Usually the best way to identify what could be blocking traffic is to boot back into regular mode, and check every program running in the system tray. Anything that is anti-virus, anti-spyware, or firewall related could be the culprit.

On macOS, this software would most likely present itself either in the menu bar at the top of the screen, or at the very bottom of System Preferences.

Once you have identified any type of antivirus, anti-spyware, or firewall software running, start by disabling the software altogether. [...] ## Updating...

University of Oregon Home Page

# How To Diagnose Internet Connectivity Problems

## Question

How can I diagnose my internet-connectivity problems?

## Solution

Diagnosing a failing internet connection can be fairly complicated. There are a number of different factors involved in the chain between the software configuration, the firmware drivers, the hardware device, the network equipment, and finally the internet service provider (ISP) uplink. If any one link in the chain is broken, the connection won't work. However all the user may experience is a web page won't load. In other words, it's not always immediately clear specifically which part is failing.
